jokes.text: Stop calling her that. / I'll tell you how she died. You know that sign that says 'Do not stand up on the roller coaster'? Yeah. She overdosed right in front of it.
jokes.setup: Homer sets up what sounds like a roller coaster accident story
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation - she overdosed near the sign, not a coaster accident
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that builds tension around a safety sign only to reveal something darker and more absurd

jokes.text: Oh. Class all the way.
jokes.setup: Learning Amber overdosed in front of a safety sign
jokes.punchline: Marge's sarcastic understated response to the trashy circumstances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge character moment - her judgmental but restrained response to Homer's trashy second wife

jokes.text: Marge, could you let it go? You won. She's dead.
jokes.setup: Marge being sarcastic about Amber's death
jokes.punchline: Homer treating his wife's death like Marge won a competition
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Homer's inability to process serious emotions appropriately, turning death into marital scorekeeping

jokes.text: a gifted cocktail waitress who hoped one day to learn computers
jokes.setup: Formal eulogy setting expectations for meaningful accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Her life goal was just 'learning computers' - vague and modest
jokes.explanation: Gentle satire of how modest ambitions sound when dressed up in formal eulogy language

jokes.text: Amber said she wouldn't let her eighth-grade education stop her from achieving her dreams. And yet, it did.
jokes.setup: Standard inspirational message about overcoming educational limitations
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction - it completely stopped her
jokes.explanation: Brutal comedic timing - builds up inspirational trope then immediately deflates it with blunt honesty

jokes.text: Bart! Do you want to leave the funeral early?! Do you?! Yes. Of course.
jokes.setup: Marge asks rhetorical disciplinary question expecting 'no'
jokes.punchline: Bart gives honest answer that derails her authority
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart deflating adult authority by taking rhetorical questions literally

jokes.text: Your name was Amber, just like the alert...
jokes.setup: Bart writing a funeral poem about Amber
jokes.punchline: Comparing her name to Amber Alert (missing children emergency system)
jokes.explanation: Dark wordplay connecting her name to emergency alert system - inappropriate but clever

jokes.text: Thanks a lot. I lost my place. Um... Ah, here it is. 'The end.'
jokes.setup: Bart being interrupted during his poem reading
jokes.punchline: His 'lost place' was just the two-word ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax - builds up the drama of losing his place only to reveal the minimal content left

jokes.text: Everyone relax, huh? I know the Heimlich. I learned it a few years ago. It was 1997, and Titanic was proving unsinkable at the box office...
jokes.setup: Medical emergency requiring immediate Heimlich maneuver
jokes.punchline: Homer launches into irrelevant movie box office anecdote
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer move - turning urgent situation into rambling tangent about pop culture

jokes.text: Somebody do me!
jokes.setup: Someone else choking and needing help during Homer's story
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea while Homer continues his irrelevant anecdote
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of the Heimlich situation while Homer remains oblivious

jokes.text: First, let me assure you that Bart's antics are perfectly normal for a seven-year-old. Actually, he's ten. Oh, dear. Dear, dear, dear, dear, dear.
jokes.setup: Doctor reassuring parents about normal seven-year-old behavior
jokes.punchline: Learning Bart is ten makes the behavior deeply concerning
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - medical reassurance becomes alarm when age is corrected

jokes.text: Spare us your medical mumbo jumbo. Just give us the pills. Clear-cut his brain down to the nub.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting more thoughtful treatment approach
jokes.punchline: Homer wants aggressive pharmaceutical brain modification
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual attitude toward extreme medical intervention on his son's brain

jokes.text: Drumming demands energy and concentration, one of which Bart has,
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining how drumming requires both energy and concentration
jokes.punchline: Trailing off because Bart clearly lacks concentration
jokes.explanation: Nice understated way of acknowledging Bart's obvious limitation while trying to be diplomatic

jokes.text: Whoa! Trippy!
jokes.setup: Bart experiencing drumming for first time
jokes.punchline: His surprisingly mellow, hippie-like reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat showing Bart's unexpected calm response to music therapy

jokes.text: Hey, kid, why don't you watch where you're drumming?! Sorry, White Stripes. No hard feelings? Let's kick his ass!
jokes.setup: Bart apologizing politely to angry musicians
jokes.punchline: White Stripes immediately escalate to violence despite his courtesy
jokes.explanation: Quick escalation from politeness to violence, pokes fun at rock star temperament

jokes.text: Oh! Why couldn't he give up this like he gives up everything else?
jokes.setup: Homer frustrated that Bart is persistent with drumming
jokes.punchline: Wishes Bart would quit, highlighting his usual pattern of quitting
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight - Homer ironically wanting Bart's usual lack of persistence for once

jokes.text: Transport yourself to the shores of Nova Scotia
jokes.setup: Marge using white noise machine to drown out drumming
jokes.punchline: Machine offers weirdly specific Canadian maritime vacation fantasy
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific relaxation tape content that's more random than clever

jokes.text: where cod and halibut frolic in...! He's just drumming louder!
jokes.setup: Soothing fish imagery from relaxation tape
jokes.punchline: Bart drowns out the nature sounds with louder drumming
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of the noise war between Marge and Bart

jokes.text: And a circus fire!
jokes.setup: Marge adding traffic noise to combat drumming
jokes.punchline: Escalates to catastrophic circus disaster sounds
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation peak - from nature sounds to traffic to circus disaster shows Marge's desperation

jokes.text: Oh! Let me know... when and if you want sex, honey!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to communicate over the noise war
jokes.punchline: Shouting intimate marital logistics over cacophony
jokes.explanation: Awkward intimacy forced into public by the noise situation

jokes.text: Boy, get dressed! You're going to a jazz brunch as punishment for all the racket you're making! I thought you wanted me to drum. I'm sending you mixed messages. Now, get the hell outta here. I love you so much. Damn you!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to discipline Bart about drumming
jokes.punchline: Rapidly cycling through contradictory parental messages
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - contradictory emotions and messages delivered in rapid succession showing his internal confusion

jokes.text: What it is, hepcats? You want me to scat-sing the menu? Absolutely not. Oh, God bless you, sir.
jokes.setup: Jazz musician offering to perform the menu
jokes.punchline: Customer's firm rejection treated as divine blessing
jokes.explanation: Good satire of overly enthusiastic jazz performance culture meeting customer practicality

jokes.text: Hey, Bart, you want to jam with me? Okay. If you give me your omelet. Whoo-hoo! I'm a professional musician!
jokes.setup: Musician inviting Bart to play
jokes.punchline: Bart negotiates food payment and declares himself professional
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart opportunism - turns musical invitation into food negotiation and instant career claim

jokes.text: Not bad for a newbie, Bart! You just may be the second-best. In case you're wondering who the best one is, it's me.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Bart and setting up ranking system
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily clarifies his own superior position
jokes.explanation: Mild ego humor - musician can't give compliment without asserting dominance

jokes.text: That's a wrap, kids. Now go have some Jazzy Bread. That's bread that fell on the floor.
jokes.setup: Offering special 'Jazzy Bread' to kids
jokes.punchline: It's just dropped bread rebranded with jazz terminology
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - perfectly captures how venues rebrand cheap/free items with fancy names

jokes.text: I'm, uh, Defonzo 'Skinny' Palmer. This is Marcus 'Marbles' Le Marquez. / We were about to play a quick set, Yes? Lisa Simpson... Yes? would do us the honor... Yes? of sitting in... that chair in the audience. We wanna jam with your brother.
jokes.setup: Jazz legends building up to invite Lisa to perform
jokes.punchline: Just want her to sit and watch while they play with Bart
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection - builds Lisa's hopes with formal invitation language only to deflate them completely

jokes.text: I was jamming with Skinny Turner. And they want me to join their trio! Woo-hoo! What's next? A contract with Groove-tone Records? Take the G-train, baby. Groove-tone!
jokes.setup: Bart excited about joining jazz trio
jokes.punchline: Immediately fantasizing about record deals and using forced jazz slang
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's tendency to immediately jump to grandiose conclusions and adopt new personas

jokes.text: 'Cause I've wanted to jam with jazz musicians all my life. / How would Bart like it if I just hopped on his skateboard and instantly became great?
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining her lifelong jazz passion
jokes.punchline: Imagining the reverse scenario with Bart's skateboarding
jokes.explanation: Good character insight showing Lisa's sense of injustice at Bart's natural talent in her area

jokes.text: Ah... whoa...
jokes.setup: Lisa actually trying to skateboard like Bart
jokes.punchline: Immediately failing and presumably falling off
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy callback showing Lisa literally attempting her hypothetical scenario

jokes.text: I need you to teach me all about the world of juzz. It's jazz! Jazz! You don't even know the name of the thing
jokes.setup: Bart asking Lisa to teach him about jazz
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'jazz' as 'juzz' proving he knows nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Bart's ignorance despite his success - can't even pronounce the genre name correctly

jokes.text: Oh-- I always wanted a jazz nickname. Fine. We'll call you, uh... Downbeat. 'Cause you're bringin' us down. That is the most unfair... Downbeat, Downbeat, please.
jokes.setup: Bart excited about getting a cool jazz nickname
jokes.punchline: Gets insulting name 'Downbeat' because he's depressing them
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - jazz nicknames are usually cool but his reflects his negative effect on the group

jokes.text: We're shootin' the breeze with Tic Tock. Oh, man, I am so juzzed.
jokes.setup: Musicians giving Bart the nickname 'Tic Tock'
jokes.punchline: Bart still mispronounces 'jazzed' as 'juzzed'
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Bart hasn't learned proper pronunciation despite his involvement in jazz

jokes.text: Bart, a rising star like you needs a manager, and Gil's your guy! Oh, I'm a jazz daddy from way back. With the skit-skat skittily boom-de-boom, and the ding-dong daddy... Is that jazz? That's jazz, right? Cha-cha-cha with the curly fries.
jokes.setup: Gil claiming to be a jazz expert and manager
jokes.punchline: His 'jazz' knowledge is nonsense sounds and ends with 'curly fries'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gil character moment - desperate salesman making up expertise and ending with absurd food reference

jokes.text: I like you more, now that other people like you.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing newfound affection for Bart
jokes.punchline: Admits his love is conditional on public approval
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest and perfectly Homer - reveals shallow parental love based on reflected glory rather than unconditional acceptance

jokes.text: It's mergatronic, Daddy-Ho. / Bart, does that even mean anything? / Xavier Cugat!
jokes.setup: Bart using made-up jazz terminology, Lisa questioning it
jokes.punchline: Homer responds with random bandleader name as if it's relevant
jokes.explanation: Absurd exchange where everyone speaks nonsense jazz-speak with false confidence

jokes.text: Mom, I'm gonna lose it. Bart's on the cover of both local jazz magazines: 'Blowin'' and 'The Sugar Sheet'. Here's the only thing I've been on the cover of.
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about Bart's jazz magazine covers
jokes.punchline: Shows her own pathetic magazine cover (presumably something embarrassing)
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry humor with Lisa's jealousy over Bart's success in her domain

jokes.text: Yeah, you know what's even cuter? Picking one without talking to me.
jokes.setup: Lisa and Marge looking at cute puppies
jokes.punchline: Worker sarcastically notes they should discuss adoption decisions
jokes.explanation: Mild workplace humor about customers making decisions without consulting staff

jokes.text: Who's gonna get neutered tomorrow? You are! Oh, yes you are.
jokes.setup: Worker using baby talk voice with puppy
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully announcing surgical castration in cute voice
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor contrasting cute pet talk with harsh veterinary reality

jokes.text: Lisa Simpson... you doomed me. I did? How? By choosing the cuter puppy. You picked looks over personality, youth over experience, no history of rabies over unknown rabies status. And now I'm going to die.
jokes.setup: Older dog blaming Lisa for its fate
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly absurd criteria ending with unknown rabies status
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from reasonable complaints to absurd rabies concerns, with talking dog adding surreal element

jokes.text: You suuuuuck! You suuuuuck!
jokes.setup: Dog making his case to Lisa about unfair selection
jokes.punchline: Devolves into simple, drawn-out insults
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation from articulate complaint to childish name-calling

jokes.text: You suuuuuck! You suuuuuck! / Ah, thanks, sweetie. / You suuuuuck!
jokes.setup: Dog insulting Lisa repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Lisa mishearing or misinterpreting the insults as affection
jokes.explanation: Absurdist moment but the repetition feels more forced than funny

jokes.text: Yeah, he'll be in God's Dumpster tomorrow
jokes.setup: Discussing what happens to unwanted animals
jokes.punchline: Euphemistically calls euthanasia 'God's Dumpster'
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant euphemism for pet euthanasia that's both offensive and memorable

jokes.text: because he knows my darkest secret: I like putting animals to sleep.
jokes.setup: Worker explaining why another dog needs to be euthanized
jokes.punchline: Admits to enjoying the killing aspect of his job
jokes.explanation: Disturbing character revelation that explains his casual attitude toward animal death

jokes.text: And if you get hungry, there are rats living in the Christmas decorations.
jokes.setup: Lisa hiding rescued animals in her room
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions rats in stored holiday decorations as food source
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing the practical realities of Lisa's animal rescue operation

jokes.text: Shut up! I just said 'shut up.' I'll punish myself by going upstairs.
jokes.setup: Lisa hearing animal noises and trying to cover
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly explains her outburst and gives herself punishment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's guilt over her secret and over-explanation

jokes.text: Uh... not smoking reefer. Uh, that's right. We all not smoking reefer.
jokes.setup: Adults finding Bart with jazz musicians
jokes.punchline: Awkward denial about marijuana use with poor grammar
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward denial humor with jazz musician stereotype about drug use

jokes.text: I was a great drummer, and now I'm nothing, just like Phil Collins.
jokes.setup: Bart lamenting his lost drumming ability
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to Phil Collins (successful drummer turned pop star)
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that doesn't quite land - Phil Collins was very successful, not 'nothing'

jokes.text: And what about my new job as a Mexican wrestler? Beware of Taco Belly. Whoo! Eat your heart...
jokes.setup: Family discussion about their various problems
jokes.punchline: Homer randomly brings up unrelated wrestler persona with food-based threat
jokes.explanation: Random Homer tangent that doesn't connect well to the main story threads

jokes.text: Uh, yes, I would like a bird because I'm often
jokes.setup: Someone calling about adopting a bird
jokes.punchline: Cuts off before completing the presumably weird reason
jokes.explanation: Incomplete joke that doesn't deliver a clear punchline

jokes.text: Can I, uh, can I borrow that ostrich?
jokes.setup: Animal Control threatening to round up all animals
jokes.punchline: Someone casually asks to borrow an ostrich like it's normal
jokes.explanation: Simple absurd request treating exotic animal like everyday item

jokes.text: Not if I can help it, Lisa. Do you have an idea? Uh, no. Sorry if it sounded like I did.
jokes.setup: Homer sounding determined to help save the animals
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he has no plan or ideas
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - confident posturing immediately deflated by his own honesty about incompetence

jokes.text: Sorry, Tic Tock. Your tick is on... but your tock is gone. Well, I've got a confession to make. This right arm... it's not mine. Whose is it? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Musicians telling Bart his drumming career is over using his nickname
jokes.punchline: Bart reveals his arm isn't his own but doesn't know whose it is
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with the nickname followed by absurd revelation about mysterious arm ownership

jokes.text: It's like a potato chip full of shame, going down my throat sideways.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to describe feeling empathy for the first time
jokes.punchline: Compares emotional guilt to uncomfortable food experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific metaphor for guilt that perfectly captures Bart's way of processing unfamiliar emotions through physical sensations

jokes.text: You're my mother. How can you say that?
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Bart do something nice for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Bart's mock outrage at the suggestion of sibling kindness
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Bart's resistance to doing the right thing even when suggested by his mother

jokes.text: Gooey Martin, Willie Mimms, Drop Jaws Turner, Sketch Friendly, Tootsie Childs, Sammy Biltmore, No-Talent Jones, Anwar Benitez, Bossy Marmalade, Bad-Check Mazursky, Ray-Ray Takamura, Shaky Premise, Bootsy Croutonne, Richard Sakai, The Pre-Marital Sextet, C.S.I. Miami, D.W. Jitters, The Chubb Group, Cantaloupe St. Pierre and many, many, more funny names!
jokes.setup: List of jazz performers for the benefit concert
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd fake jazz names mixed with random references
jokes.explanation: Creative but overlong list of fake jazz names that loses steam due to length but has some clever individual entries

jokes.text: Now I'm gonna go vomit to keep my weight down. That's right. That's how Krusty does it.
jokes.setup: Krusty finishing his announcement duties
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions bulimia as weight management strategy
jokes.explanation: Darkly humorous revelation about Krusty's unhealthy habits delivered with characteristic casualness

jokes.text: Jazz folk may have the smallest apartments, but they also have the biggest hearts.
jokes.setup: Bart thanking everyone at the benefit concert
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about jazz musicians being poor but kind
jokes.explanation: Nice observational humor about the stereotype of struggling but generous artists
